title: The reason Russia and China can't win at sea - Sarah Paine
summary: Sarah Paine discusses structural geopolitical and military constraints on Russian and Chinese naval power projection. The analysis addresses why certain nations face inherent disadvantages in competing at sea.
writeup: Sarah Paine examines the structural geopolitical and military constraints that limit Russian and Chinese naval power projection. The discussion frames why some nations face inherent disadvantages in competing at sea, tying maritime capability to fixed geographic and strategic conditions rather than fleet size alone. The takeaway is that these constraints are systemic, shaping the limits of both countries' ambitions on the water.
